title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds layered edge-security plan, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered edge-security plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Waf Rate Bot Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7697 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Waf Rate Bot Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, apply waf, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Waf Rate Bot Boundary, keep Waf Rate Bot Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
